title: Auricula-ursi integrifolia
intro: Auricula-ursi integrifolia, also known as Bearberry or Kinnikinnick, is a perennial shrub native to the boreal forests of North America, characterized by its small white flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its ability to treat urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its antiseptic and astringent properties. The bioactive constituents of Bearberry include arbutin, which is responsible for its antiseptic and astringent effects, as well as ursolic acid and other triterpenes, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal medicine, Bearberry is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to treat a range of urinary and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Auricula-ursi integrifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It is a type of bearberry, a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The genus name 'Auricula-ursi' is derived from the Latin words for 'bear's ear', likely due to the plant's resemblance to a bear's ear when in flower. The species name 'integrifolia' refers to the plant's entire leaves, distinguishing it from other species in the same genus. Auricula-ursi integrifolia is a member of the Ericales order and is closely related to other bearberries and blueberries.
uses: Auricula-ursi integrifolia, also known as Bearberry, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antiseptic and astringent properties. It has been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and other conditions, such as diarrhea and kidney stones. The plant contains compounds like arbutin, which may help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria. constituents: Auricula-ursi integrifolia, also known as Bearberry, contains several active constituents, including arbutin, which is a glycoside responsible for its antimicrobial properties. The plant also contains phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, and flavonoids, including quercetin. Additionally, Bearberry contains terpenoids and iridoids, which contribute to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active constituents in Bearberry may help to protect against infections and inflammation in the urinary tract. parts: Auricula-ursi integrifolia, the medicinal parts of this plant are primarily the leaves and stems. The leaves are rich in arbutin, a compound that has been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and other conditions. preparations: Auricula-ursi integrifolia is a plant that has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in traditional European folk remedies. Herbal preparations of Auricula-ursi integrifolia, also known as bearberry, have been used to treat urinary tract infections and other issues related to the bladder and kidneys. side_effects: Auricula-ursi integrifolia, also known as bearberry, is a plant used in herbal medicine for various purposes, but it can have some side effects. The most common side effects of bearberry include gastrointestinal problems such as diarrhea, nausea, and stomach upset. In some cases, bearberry may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, which can lead to serious health complications. Additionally, bearberry may cause allergic reactions in some individuals, including skin irritation and respiratory problems. Pregnant and breastfeeding women should also exercise caution when using bearberry due to the potential risks to their health and the health of their babies.
